text: The equation of normal to \( y=f(x) \) at \( (x_1,y_1) \) is:
explanation: Normal slope \( = -\frac{1}{f'(x_1)} \). Normal: \( y-y_1 = -\frac{1}{f'(x_1)}(x-x_1) \).
name: Equation of Normal
subject: maths

text: The motional EMF induced in a rod of length \(L\) moving with velocity \(v\) in field \(B\) is:
explanation: Motional EMF: \( \varepsilon = BLv\sin\theta \). Maximum when \(\vec{v}\perp\vec{B}\perp L\): \( \varepsilon = BLv \). Due to Lorentz force on free electrons.
name: Motional EMF
subject: phy

text: The photoelectric effect conclusively demonstrated that:
options: Light energy is quantised (comes in packets called photons)
explanation: Photoelectric effect: showed light energy is quantised (\(E = h\nu\)), not continuous. Wave theory couldn't explain the existence of threshold frequency and instantaneous emission.
name: Conclusion from Photoelectric Effect
subject: phy

text: From a pack of 52 cards, probability of drawing a king is:
explanation: 4 kings in 52 cards: \( P = \frac{4}{52} = \frac{1}{13} \)
name: Card Probability
subject: maths

text: The number of real roots of \( x^2 + |x| + 1 = 0 \) is:
explanation: For all real \(x\): \( x^2 \geq 0,\ |x| \geq 0 \), so \( x^2+|x|+1 \geq 1 > 0 \). No real roots.
name: No Real Roots
subject: maths

text: The condition for \( lx+my+n=0 \) to be tangent to ellipse \( \frac{x^2}{a^2}+\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1 \) is:
explanation: Tangent condition to ellipse: \( a^2l^2+b^2m^2=n^2 \)
name: Tangent Condition to Ellipse
subject: maths

text: The law of conservation of mass states that in a chemical reaction:
options: Mass of products > mass of reactants
Mass of reactants = mass of products
Mass is always lost as energy
explanation: Lavoisier's Law: Total mass of reactants = Total mass of products. Matter is neither created nor destroyed.
name: Conservation of Mass
subject: che

text: Which reagent is used to oxidise primary alcohol to aldehyde without over-oxidising to carboxylic acid?
explanation: PCC (CH₂Cl₂, mild): oxidises 1° alcohol → aldehyde (stops there). Strong oxidants (KMnO₄, K₂Cr₂O₇) → carboxylic acid.
name: Selective Oxidation to Aldehyde
subject: che

text: For a system \( AX = B \), if \( |A| \neq 0 \), the unique solution is:
explanation: Multiplying \( AX = B \) by \( A^{-1} \) on left: \( X = A^{-1}B \)
name: Cramer's Rule / Matrix Method
subject: maths

text: The resonant frequency of an LCR circuit is:
explanation: Resonant frequency: \( f_0 = \frac{1}{2\pi\sqrt{LC}} \). \( \omega_0 = 1/\sqrt{LC} \). Used in radio tuning circuits.
name: Resonant Frequency
subject: phy

text: The coefficient of restitution \(e\) for a perfectly elastic collision is:
explanation: \( e = \frac{\text{relative speed of separation}}{\text{relative speed of approach}} \). Perfectly elastic: \(e=1\), Perfectly inelastic: \(e=0\).
name: Coefficient of Restitution
subject: phy

text: de Broglie wavelength of a particle with momentum \(p\) is:
explanation: de Broglie relation: \( \lambda = \frac{h}{p} = \frac{h}{mv} \). Wave-particle duality.
name: de Broglie Wavelength
subject: che

text: The product formula \( 2\sin A \cos B = \)
explanation: Product-to-Sum: \( 2\sin A\cos B = \sin(A+B) + \sin(A-B) \)
name: Product-to-Sum
subject: maths

text: \( \int x^n\,dx = \)
explanation: Power Rule for integration: \( \int x^n\,dx = \frac{x^{n+1}}{n+1}+C,\ n\neq -1 \)
name: Power Rule Integration
subject: maths

text: Which of the following is the correct order of acidic strength of hydrocarbons?
explanation: Acidic strength: alkyne (sp C, more electronegative) > alkene (sp²) > alkane (sp³). pKa: alkyne ≈ 25, alkene ≈ 44, alkane ≈ 50.
name: Acidity of Hydrocarbons
subject: che

text: The angular magnification of an astronomical telescope is:
explanation: Astronomical telescope: \( M = f_o/f_e \). Large \(f_o\) and small \(f_e\) → high magnification. Length \( = f_o + f_e \) (image at infinity).
name: Telescope Magnification
subject: phy

text: Using induction, \( 4^n - 1 \) is divisible by:
explanation: \( 4^n - 1 = (4-1)(4^{n-1}+\cdots+1) = 3 \times (\text{integer}) \). Always divisible by 3.
name: Divisibility by Induction
subject: maths

text: Electrophilic substitution (EAS) on benzene: the rate-determining step involves:
options: Loss of H⁺ from sigma complex
Addition of electrophile to all carbons
explanation: EAS mechanism: (1) Formation of π complex, (2) Formation of σ complex (arenium ion, slow, RDS), (3) Loss of H⁺ (fast) to restore aromaticity.
name: EAS Rate-Determining Step
subject: che

text: The process of heating carbonate or hydrated oxide ores in absence of air to remove CO₂ or water is:
explanation: Calcination: \( \text{CaCO}_3 \to \text{CaO} + \text{CO}_2 \) or \( \text{Al}_2\text{O}_3 \cdot 2\text{H}_2\text{O} \to \text{Al}_2\text{O}_3 + 2\text{H}_2\text{O} \).
name: Calcination
subject: che

text: Standard deviation is the _____ of variance:
explanation: \( \sigma = \sqrt{\text{Variance}} \). SD is positive square root of variance.
name: SD and Variance Relation
subject: maths

text: The phenomenon of movement of colloidal particles under electric field is called:
explanation: Electrophoresis: movement of charged colloidal particles in an electric field. Electro-osmosis: movement of dispersion medium.
name: Electrophoresis
subject: che

text: The hybridisation of carbon in ethyne (\( \text{C}_2\text{H}_2 \)) is:
explanation: In \(\text{C}_2\text{H}_2\) (acetylene): triple bond between C atoms → \(sp\) hybridisation, linear shape (180°).
name: Hybridisation in Ethyne
subject: che

text: The kinetic energy of rolling without slipping is:
explanation: Rolling KE = translational + rotational: \( KE = \frac{1}{2}mv^2 + \frac{1}{2}I\omega^2 \). For solid sphere: \( KE = \frac{7}{10}mv^2 \).
name: Rolling Kinetic Energy
subject: phy

text: The standard enthalpy of formation (\( \Delta H^\circ_f \)) of an element in its standard state is:
explanation: By definition, \( \Delta H^\circ_f = 0 \) for any element in its standard state (e.g., O₂(g), C(graphite)).
name: Standard Enthalpy of Element
subject: che

text: In a head-on elastic collision between equal masses where one is at rest, after collision:
options: First stops, second moves with first's velocity
explanation: Equal mass elastic collision: first body stops, second moves with velocity of first. Velocities exchange completely.
name: Elastic Collision Equal Masses
subject: phy

text: \( \lim_{x \to 0} \frac{\sin x}{x} = \)
explanation: Standard limit: \( \lim_{x\to 0}\frac{\sin x}{x} = 1 \) (x in radians). Most important limit formula.
name: sin x / x Limit
subject: maths

text: The transistor in common emitter configuration has:
explanation: CE configuration: \( \beta = I_C/I_B \gg 1 \). Both current gain and voltage gain > 1 → power amplification. Most used transistor configuration.
name: Common Emitter Current Gain
subject: phy

text: \( \hat{i}\times\hat{j} = \)
explanation: \( \hat{i}\times\hat{j}=\hat{k},\ \hat{j}\times\hat{k}=\hat{i},\ \hat{k}\times\hat{i}=\hat{j} \). Cyclic order.
name: Unit Vector Cross Products
subject: maths

text: The trace of matrix \( A = \begin{pmatrix} 2 & 3 \\ 1 & 5 \end{pmatrix} \) is:
explanation: Trace = sum of diagonal elements \( = 2 + 5 = 7 \)
name: Trace of Matrix
subject: maths

text: The root mean square speed of gas molecules is:
explanation: \( u_{rms} = \sqrt{\frac{3RT}{M}} \). Order: \( u_{mp} < u_{avg} < u_{rms} \).
name: RMS Speed
subject: che

text: The sum of slopes of lines \( ax^2+2hxy+by^2=0 \) is:
explanation: If \(m_1,m_2\) are slopes: \( m_1+m_2 = -\frac{2h}{b} \) and \( m_1m_2 = \frac{a}{b} \) (Vieta's).
name: Sum of Slopes
subject: maths

text: If \( \alpha, \beta \) are roots of \( 2x^2 - 5x + 3 = 0 \), then \( \alpha \cdot \beta = \)
explanation: Product of roots \( = \frac{c}{a} = \frac{3}{2} \)
name: Product of Roots
subject: maths

text: Aldehydes and ketones are distinguished by Tollens' reagent (ammoniacal AgNO₃). Aldehydes give:
explanation: Tollens' test: RCHO + 2[Ag(NH₃)₂]⁺ + 2OH⁻ → RCOOH + 2Ag↓ + 4NH₃. Silver mirror formed. Ketones give no reaction.
name: Tollens' Test
subject: che

text: The RMS value of an AC voltage \( V = V_0\sin(\omega t) \) is:
explanation: RMS value: \( V_{rms} = V_0/\sqrt{2} \). For Indian household supply: \( V_{rms}=220\,\text{V} \Rightarrow V_0\approx 311\,\text{V} \).
name: RMS Voltage
subject: phy

text: The Finkelstein reaction involves exchange of halides using:
explanation: Finkelstein reaction: R–Cl + NaI (acetone) → R–I + NaCl. NaCl precipitates (insoluble in acetone), driving equilibrium forward.
name: Finkelstein Reaction
subject: che

text: The equation \( \frac{x^2}{16}+\frac{y^2}{9}=1 \). Here \( a=4, b=3 \), so \( c = \)
explanation: \( c^2 = a^2-b^2 = 16-9 = 7 \Rightarrow c = \sqrt{7} \)
name: Foci of Ellipse
subject: maths

text: The domain of \( f(x) = \sqrt{x - 3} \) is:
explanation: For \(\sqrt{x-3}\) to be defined, \( x - 3 \geq 0 \Rightarrow x \geq 3 \)
name: Domain of Square Root
subject: maths

text: The equation of normal to circle \( x^2+y^2=r^2 \) at point \((x_1,y_1)\) is:
explanation: The normal to a circle at any point passes through the center (origin). Equation: \( \frac{x}{x_1}=\frac{y}{y_1} \) or \( x_1y - y_1x=0 \).
name: Normal to Circle
subject: maths

text: The modulus-amplitude form of \( z = -\sqrt{3} + i \) is:
explanation: \( r = \sqrt{3+1} = 2 \). Angle in 2nd quadrant: \( \theta = \pi - \frac{\pi}{6} = \frac{5\pi}{6} \)
name: Modulus-Amplitude Form
subject: maths

text: \( \cosh 2x = \)
explanation: All three are valid: \( \cosh 2x = 2\cosh^2x-1 = 1+2\sinh^2x = \cosh^2x+\sinh^2x \)
name: cosh 2x Identities
subject: maths

text: The first law of thermodynamics is:
options: Heat flows from hot to cold
explanation: First Law: \( \Delta U = Q - W \). Internal energy change = heat absorbed − work done by system.
name: First Law of Thermodynamics
subject: phy

text: Kirchhoff's voltage law (KVL) states:
options: Current is constant around a loop
Sum of all voltages (EMFs and drops) around any closed loop = 0
Voltage is same across all elements
explanation: KVL (loop rule): \( \sum V = \sum EMF - \sum IR = 0 \). Based on conservation of energy.
name: Kirchhoff's Voltage Law
subject: phy

text: The vector equation of line through point \(\vec{a}\) in direction \(\vec{b}\) is:
explanation: Vector equation of line: \( \vec{r} = \vec{a} + \lambda\vec{b} \), where \(\lambda \in \mathbb{R}\).
name: Vector Equation of Line
subject: maths

text: The number of lone pairs on the central atom of \( \text{XeF}_4 \) is:
explanation: Xe in \(\text{XeF}_4\): 4 bond pairs + 2 lone pairs → \(sp^3d^2\) → square planar shape.
name: Lone Pairs in XeF₄
subject: che

explanation: \( \tan^{-1}x + \cot^{-1}x = \frac{\pi}{2} \) for all real \(x\).
name: tan⁻¹ + cot⁻¹ Identity
subject: maths

text: The magnifying power of a simple microscope at near point (D=25 cm) is:
explanation: Simple microscope: \( M = 1 + D/f \) (image at near point). \( M = D/f \) (image at infinity). Near point \(D = 25\) cm.
name: Magnifying Power of Simple Microscope
subject: phy

text: A bag has 3 red and 5 blue balls. Two are drawn without replacement. \( P(\text{both red}) = \)
name: Without Replacement
subject: maths

text: The inverse of \( A = \begin{pmatrix} 2 & 1 \\ 5 & 3 \end{pmatrix} \) is:
explanation: \( |A| = 6-5=1 \). \( A^{-1} = \frac{1}{|A|}\begin{pmatrix}d & -b \\ -c & a\end{pmatrix} = \begin{pmatrix}3 & -1 \\ -5 & 2\end{pmatrix} \)
name: 2x2 Inverse
subject: maths

text: An ideal solution obeys Raoult's law over the entire concentration range and has:
explanation: Ideal solution: \( \Delta H_{mix} = 0 \), \( \Delta V_{mix} = 0 \). Example: benzene + toluene.
name: Ideal Solution Properties
subject: che

text: The radius of gyration \(k\) is defined by:
explanation: Radius of gyration: \( k = \sqrt{I/M} \), so \( I = Mk^2 \). It represents the effective distance of the mass from the axis.
name: Radius of Gyration
subject: phy

text: The partial fraction form of \( \frac{px+q}{(x-a)(x-b)} \) is:
explanation: For distinct linear factors, partial fractions take the form \( \frac{A}{x-a} + \frac{B}{x-b} \).
name: Partial Fraction Form - Linear Factors
subject: maths

text: The relation \( r = 4R\sin\frac{A}{2}\sin\frac{B}{2}\sin\frac{C}{2} \) connects:
explanation: This is the standard formula relating inradius \(r\) and circumradius \(R\) with half-angles.
name: r and R Relation
subject: maths

text: The general solution of \( \sin\theta = 0 \) is:
explanation: \( \sin\theta = 0 \Rightarrow \theta = n\pi \), where \( n \in \mathbb{Z} \).
name: General Solution of sinθ=0
subject: maths

text: The point dividing \( P(x_1,y_1) \) and \( Q(x_2,y_2) \) in ratio \( m:n \) internally is:
explanation: Section Formula (internal): \( \left(\frac{mx_2+nx_1}{m+n}, \frac{my_2+ny_1}{m+n}\right) \)
name: Section Formula
subject: maths

text: The troposphere extends from Earth's surface to approximately:
explanation: Troposphere: 0–12 km (approx). Contains 75% of atmosphere's mass. Weather occurs here. Temperature decreases with altitude.
name: Troposphere Height
subject: che

text: \( \frac{d}{dx}(e^x) = \)
explanation: \( \frac{d}{dx}(e^x) = e^x \). The exponential function is its own derivative.
name: Derivative of eˣ
subject: maths

text: In an electromagnetic wave, the electric and magnetic fields are:
options: Perpendicular to each other and to direction of propagation
explanation: EM waves: \( \vec{E} \perp \vec{B} \perp \vec{v} \). All three are mutually perpendicular. EM waves are transverse.
name: EM Wave Field Orientation
subject: phy

text: Condensation polymerisation involves:
options: Loss of small molecules (like H₂O, HCl) at each step
explanation: Condensation (step growth) polymerisation: monomers react with loss of small molecules (H₂O, HCl). E.g., nylon, polyester, Bakelite.
name: Condensation Polymerisation
subject: che

text: KMnO₄ in acidic medium acts as:
explanation: In acidic medium: \( \text{MnO}_4^- + 8\text{H}^+ + 5e^- \to \text{Mn}^{2+} + 4\text{H}_2\text{O} \). KMnO₄ is a powerful oxidant. Mn: +7 → +2.
name: KMnO₄ in Acidic Medium
subject: che

text: Area of triangle \(ABC\) using two sides and included angle is:
explanation: Area \( = \frac{1}{2}ab\sin C = \frac{1}{2}bc\sin A = \frac{1}{2}ca\sin B \)
name: Area of Triangle
subject: maths

text: By Descartes' rule, the maximum number of positive roots of \( x^3 - 3x^2 + 4 = 0 \) is:
explanation: Sign changes: \(+, -, +\) — 2 sign changes, so max 2 positive roots (or 0).
name: Descartes' Rule of Signs
subject: maths

text: The triple point of water (where all three phases coexist) is at:
explanation: Triple point of water: 273.16 K (0.01°C) and 611.73 Pa (≈ 0.006 atm). Unique fixed point used to define the Kelvin scale.
name: Triple Point of Water
subject: phy

text: If \( |A| = 5 \) and \( A \) is \( 3 \times 3 \), then \( |\text{adj}(A)| = \)
name: Adjoint Determinant
subject: maths

text: The pair of lines \( ax^2+2hxy+by^2=0 \) are perpendicular if:
explanation: The lines are perpendicular when \( a + b = 0 \) (sum of coefficients of \(x^2\) and \(y^2\) is zero).
name: Perpendicular Pair
subject: maths

text: The internal resistance \(r\) of a battery can be found using:
explanation: Terminal voltage: \( V = E - Ir \). When external \(R\) is connected: \( I = E/(R+r) \) and \( V = IR = E-Ir \).
name: Internal Resistance
subject: phy

text: The pair of lines \( ax^2+2hxy+by^2=0 \) coincide when:
explanation: Lines coincide (repeated) when \( h^2 = ab \) (discriminant = 0).
name: Coincident Lines
subject: maths

text: The solution of linear first-order DE \( \frac{dy}{dx}+Py=Q \) is:
explanation: After multiplying by IF: \( \frac{d}{dx}(y\cdot\text{IF}) = Q\cdot\text{IF} \Rightarrow y\cdot\text{IF} = \int Q\cdot\text{IF}\,dx+C \)
name: Linear DE Solution
subject: maths

text: The acceleration due to gravity \(g\) varies with height \(h\) above Earth's surface as:
explanation: \( g' = \frac{g}{(1+h/R)^2} \approx g\left(1 - \frac{2h}{R}\right) \) for \( h \ll R \). Decreases with height.
name: g at Height h
subject: phy

text: The transformer works on the principle of:
explanation: Transformer: mutual induction. \( V_2/V_1 = N_2/N_1 = I_1/I_2 \) (ideal). Step-up: \(N_2>N_1\). Step-down: \(N_2<N_1\).
name: Transformer Principle
subject: phy

text: Relative velocity of A with respect to B is:
explanation: Relative velocity: \( \vec{v}_{A/B} = \vec{v}_A - \vec{v}_B \). For two trains moving in same direction at \(v_1, v_2\): relative speed \(= v_1 - v_2\).
name: Relative Velocity
subject: phy

text: \( \tan^{-1}x + \tan^{-1}y = \tan^{-1}\frac{x+y}{1-xy} \) when:
explanation: This formula holds when \( xy < 1 \). When \(xy > 1\), add or subtract \(\pi\) depending on sign of \(x\).
name: Addition Formula Condition
subject: maths

text: The frequency of the \(n\)th harmonic in a closed pipe (one end closed) is:
explanation: Closed pipe: fundamental \( f_1 = v/4L \). Only odd harmonics: \( f_n = (2n-1)v/4L \). Open pipe: all harmonics present.
name: Closed Pipe Harmonics
subject: phy

text: \( \lim_{x \to 0} \frac{e^x - 1}{x} = \)
explanation: Standard exponential limit: \( \lim_{x\to 0}\frac{e^x-1}{x} = 1 \)
name: Exponential Limit
subject: maths

text: The f-block elements (lanthanides) have the electron being filled in:
explanation: Lanthanides (Ce to Lu): filling of 4f orbitals. Actinides: filling of 5f orbitals. Both are inner transition elements.
name: f-Block Orbital Filling
subject: che

text: The equation \( 2\cos^2\theta - \cos\theta - 1 = 0 \) in \([0, 2\pi]\) — the number of solutions is:
explanation: Factor: \( (2\cos\theta+1)(\cos\theta-1) = 0 \Rightarrow \cos\theta = -\frac{1}{2} \) (gives \(\frac{2\pi}{3}, \frac{4\pi}{3}\)) or \(\cos\theta=1\) (gives \(0\)). Total: 3 solutions.
name: Quadratic in cosθ
subject: maths

text: For a body on a rough horizontal surface, the limiting friction is:
explanation: Limiting static friction \( f_{s,max} = \mu_s N \). For any applied force \( F \leq f_{s,max} \): body remains static.
name: Limiting Friction
subject: phy

text: Weightlessness in a satellite is because:
options: Both satellite and person are in free fall (same centripetal acceleration)
explanation: In orbit: both person and satellite have same centripetal acceleration. Person is in continuous free fall → apparent weightlessness.
name: Weightlessness in Orbit
subject: phy

text: Beyond the elastic limit, a material shows:
explanation: Beyond elastic limit → plastic deformation (permanent). The material doesn't return to original shape on removing stress.
name: Beyond Elastic Limit
subject: phy

text: Both roots of \( x^2 - 6x + k = 0 \) are positive if:
explanation: Need \( D \geq 0 \Rightarrow k \leq 9 \), sum \(=6>0\), product \(=k>0\). So \( 0 < k \leq 9 \). Strictly positive roots: \( 0 < k < 9 \).
name: Both Roots Positive
subject: maths

text: For a solution showing positive deviation from Raoult's law:
explanation: Positive deviation: \(P > P_{Raoult}\), \(\Delta H_{mix} > 0\) (endothermic), \(\Delta V_{mix} > 0\). Example: ethanol + water.
name: Positive Deviation
subject: che

text: The value of \( \sin 30° = \)
explanation: Standard value: \( \sin 30° = \frac{1}{2} \)
name: sin 30°
subject: maths

text: Walli's formula: \( \int_0^{\pi/2}\sin^m x\cos^n x\,dx \) involves the pattern of multiplying down by \(2\) at the end when both \(m\) and \(n\) are:
explanation: When both \(m\) and \(n\) are even, Walli's formula requires an additional factor of \(\frac{\pi}{2}\).
name: Walli's Formula
subject: maths

text: The equation of a plane in normal form is:
explanation: Normal form of plane: \( lx+my+nz=p \), where \(l,m,n\) are direction cosines of normal and \(p>0\).
name: Plane Normal Form
subject: maths

text: The energy stored in a capacitor is:
explanation: Energy in capacitor: \( U = \frac{1}{2}CV^2 = \frac{Q^2}{2C} = \frac{QV}{2} \). Stored as electric field energy.
name: Energy in Capacitor
subject: phy

text: The moment of inertia of a ring about its diameter is:
explanation: Ring (axis through centre perpendicular): \( I = MR^2 \). Ring (diameter): \( I = \frac{MR^2}{2} \) (by perpendicular axis theorem: \( MR^2 = I_x + I_y = 2I_{diameter} \)).
name: MOI of Ring about Diameter
subject: phy

text: The bandwidth of an AM signal with message frequency \(f_m\) is:
explanation: AM bandwidth: \( 2f_m \). Sidebands at \( f_c \pm f_m \). For voice (up to 4 kHz), AM bandwidth = 8 kHz.
name: AM Bandwidth
subject: phy

text: \( \frac{d}{dx}(\sin x) = \)
explanation: \( \frac{d}{dx}(\sin x) = \cos x \)
name: Derivative of sin
subject: maths

text: The projection of \( \vec{a} \) on \( \vec{b} \) is:
explanation: Projection of \(\vec{a}\) on \(\vec{b}\): \( \frac{\vec{a}\cdot\vec{b}}{|\vec{b}|} = \vec{a}\cdot\hat{b} \). Both forms are equivalent.
name: Projection of Vector
subject: maths

text: The time period of a satellite orbiting at height \(h\) is:
explanation: \( T = \frac{2\pi r}{v_o} = \frac{2\pi r}{\sqrt{GM/r}} = 2\pi\sqrt{\frac{r^3}{GM}} \). This is Kepler's 3rd law.
name: Orbital Period
subject: phy

text: \( \cos 3\theta = \)
explanation: Triple angle formula: \( \cos 3\theta = 4\cos^3\theta - 3\cos\theta \)
name: Triple Angle - cos
subject: maths

text: Bleaching powder contains:
explanation: Bleaching powder: Ca(OCl)Cl (mixed salt of Ca²⁺ with OCl⁻ and Cl⁻). Formula: CaOCl₂ or Ca(OCl)Cl. Made by Cl₂ + Ca(OH)₂.
name: Bleaching Powder
subject: che

text: For a homogeneous equilibrium, the equilibrium constant expression excludes:
explanation: Activities of pure solids and pure liquids are taken as 1 and excluded from K expressions.
name: K Expression Convention
subject: che

text: The permittivity of free space \(\epsilon_0\) has value:
explanation: \( \epsilon_0 = 8.85\times10^{-12}\,\text{C}^2/(\text{N m}^2) \). Note: \( k = 1/4\pi\epsilon_0 \approx 9\times10^9\,\text{N m}^2/\text{C}^2 \).
name: Permittivity of Free Space
subject: phy

text: Rolle's theorem requires \(f\) to be continuous on \([a,b]\), differentiable on \((a,b)\), and:
explanation: Rolle's Theorem: if additionally \( f(a) = f(b) \), then \( \exists\, c\in(a,b) \) such that \( f'(c)=0 \).
name: Rolle's Theorem Condition
subject: maths

text: \( \lim_{x \to 0} \frac{\log(1+x)}{x} = \)
explanation: Standard logarithmic limit: \( \lim_{x\to 0}\frac{\ln(1+x)}{x} = 1 \)
name: Logarithmic Limit
subject: maths

text: For the reaction \( 2\text{SO}_2 + \text{O}_2 \rightleftharpoons 2\text{SO}_3 \), if \(K_c = 4\) for the forward reaction, then \(K_c\) for the reverse reaction is:
explanation: For reverse reaction: \( K_c' = \frac{1}{K_c} = \frac{1}{4} = 0.25 \).
name: K for Reverse Reaction
subject: che

text: The coordinates of a point dividing segment \(AB\) (\(A=(x_1,y_1,z_1)\), \(B=(x_2,y_2,z_2)\)) in ratio \(m:n\) internally are:
explanation: Section formula in 3D (internal): \( \left(\frac{mx_2+nx_1}{m+n}, \frac{my_2+ny_1}{m+n}, \frac{mz_2+nz_1}{m+n}\right) \)
name: 3D Section Formula
subject: maths

text: The maximum value of \( f(x) = \sin x + \cos x \) is:
explanation: \( f(x) = \sqrt{2}\sin(x+\frac{\pi}{4}) \). Maximum \( = \sqrt{2} \) when \( x = \frac{\pi}{4} \).
name: Maximum of sin+cos
subject: maths

text: The standard equation of an ellipse with semi-major axis \(a\) along x-axis is:
explanation: Ellipse: \( \frac{x^2}{a^2}+\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1 \) with \(a>b>0\). Foci at \( (\pm c,0) \) where \( c^2=a^2-b^2 \).
name: Ellipse Standard Form
subject: maths

text: The term independent of \(x\) in \( \left(x + \frac{1}{x}\right)^6 \) is:
name: Term Independent of x
subject: maths

text: \( \int e^x\,dx = \)
explanation: \( \int e^x\,dx = e^x+C \)
name: Integral of eˣ
subject: maths

text: In \( \frac{3x+5}{(x-1)(x+2)} = \frac{A}{x-1} + \frac{B}{x+2} \), the value of \( A \) is:
explanation: Set \(x=1\): \( 3+5 = A(3) \Rightarrow A = \frac{8}{3} \)
name: Finding Constant A
subject: maths

text: Electron affinity is the energy change when:
options: A neutral atom loses an electron
A neutral gaseous atom gains one electron
explanation: Electron affinity (EA): energy released when a neutral gaseous atom gains an electron to form anion. Cl has highest EA.
name: Electron Affinity
subject: che

text: Hell-Volhard-Zelinsky (HVZ) reaction introduces halogen at _____ position of carboxylic acids:
explanation: HVZ reaction: RCOOH + X₂ (PCl₃/PBr₃ catalyst) → α-halocarboxylic acid. X introduced at α-carbon of the acid.
name: HVZ Reaction
subject: che

text: The equation of line through \((x_1,y_1)\) with slope \(m\) is:
explanation: Point-slope form: \( y - y_1 = m(x - x_1) \)
name: Point-Slope Form
subject: maths

text: The sphere with center \((a,b,c)\) and radius \(r\) has equation:
explanation: Sphere equation: \( (x-a)^2+(y-b)^2+(z-c)^2=r^2 \)
name: Equation of Sphere
subject: maths

text: The property \( \int_a^b f(x)\,dx = \int_a^b f(a+b-x)\,dx \) is used to evaluate:
options: Integrals that are difficult to solve directly
explanation: This \(x \to a+b-x\) substitution simplifies integrals like \( \int_0^{\pi} \frac{x\sin x}{1+\cos^2 x}dx \).
name: King's Property
subject: maths

text: The density of a crystal is given by:
explanation: Density: \( \rho = \frac{Z \times M}{N_A \times a^3} \), where \(Z\) = atoms/cell, \(M\) = molar mass, \(a\) = edge length.
name: Density of Crystal
subject: che

text: The pressure of an ideal gas is given by kinetic theory as:
explanation: Kinetic theory pressure: \( P = \frac{1}{3}\rho\overline{v^2} = \frac{1}{3}\rho v_{rms}^2 \). This derivation assumes elastic collisions and random motion.
name: Kinetic Theory Pressure Formula
subject: phy

text: The number of diagonals of a polygon with \(n\) sides is:
explanation: Total lines \( = \binom{n}{2} \), minus \(n\) sides: diagonals \( = \frac{n(n-1)}{2} - n = \frac{n(n-3)}{2} \)
name: Diagonals of Polygon
subject: maths

text: The number of ways of selecting at least one from 3 items is:
explanation: Total subsets \( = 2^3 = 8 \). Excluding empty set: \( 2^3 - 1 = 7 \)
name: Select At Least One
subject: maths

text: A catalyst increases the rate of reaction by:
options: Providing an alternative pathway with lower activation energy
explanation: Catalyst provides alternate reaction pathway with lower \(E_a\). More molecules have enough energy to react.
name: How Catalyst Works
subject: che

text: The diameter of the \(n\)th dark ring in Newton's rings is:
explanation: Newton's rings dark ring: \( D_n = 2\sqrt{n\lambda R} \), where \(R\) = radius of curvature of lens. \(D_n^2 \propto n \) (linear with \(n\)).
name: Newton's Rings Diameter
subject: phy

text: If \( P(A)=\frac{1}{2}, P(B)=\frac{1}{3} \) and \(A, B\) are independent, then \( P(A \cap B) = \)
explanation: \( P(A \cap B) = P(A) \cdot P(B) = \frac{1}{2} \cdot \frac{1}{3} = \frac{1}{6} \)
name: Independent Events Intersection
subject: maths

text: If two roots of \( x^3 - px^2 + qx - r = 0 \) are equal, say \( \alpha, \alpha, \beta \), then which condition must hold?
explanation: From Vieta's: \( 2\alpha+\beta=p \), \( \alpha^2+2\alpha\beta=q \), \( \alpha^2\beta=r \). When all three equal: \( p^3=27r \). All conditions can hold simultaneously.
name: Equal Roots in Cubic
subject: maths

text: The Product Rule: \( \frac{d}{dx}(uv) = \)
explanation: Product Rule: \( (uv)' = u'v + uv' \)
name: Product Rule
subject: maths

text: Which measure of dispersion is least affected by extreme values?
explanation: Quartile deviation uses only middle 50% of data (Q1 and Q3), so it's least affected by extreme values.
name: Robust Measure
subject: maths

text: In SN2 reaction, the nucleophile attacks:
options: Same side as the leaving group
Opposite side (backside attack) to the leaving group
explanation: SN2: bimolecular, one-step, backside attack (180°). Causes Walden inversion (inversion of configuration at chiral centre).
name: SN2 Mechanism
subject: che

text: The area of the hysteresis loop represents:
options: Energy dissipated per cycle as heat
explanation: Hysteresis loop area = energy loss per unit volume per cycle (as heat). Large area → high energy loss. Small area (soft material) → low loss, preferred for transformers/AC applications.
name: Hysteresis Loop Area
subject: phy

text: The number of terms in the expansion of \( (a+b+c)^n \) is:
name: Trinomial Expansion Terms
subject: maths

text: The coefficient of performance (COP) of a refrigerator is:
explanation: COP of refrigerator: \( COP = Q_L/W = T_C/(T_H - T_C) \) for Carnot refrigerator. Higher COP = more efficient cooling.
name: COP of Refrigerator
subject: phy

text: If \( f(x+y) = f(x) + f(y) \) for all \(x, y\) and \(f(1) = 3\), then \(f(5) = \)
explanation: This is Cauchy's functional equation: \( f(x) = kx \). \( f(1)=3 \Rightarrow k=3 \). So \( f(5) = 15 \)
name: Cauchy Functional Equation
subject: maths

text: The EMF of a cell is given by:
explanation: \( E_{cell} = E_{cathode} - E_{anode} \) (reduction potentials). For spontaneous cell: \(E_{cell} > 0\).
name: EMF of Cell
subject: che

text: The degree of \( \left(\frac{dy}{dx}\right)^3 + 2\frac{dy}{dx} + y = 0 \) is:
explanation: Degree = power of highest-order derivative (after making it polynomial). Here \( (y')^3 \) → degree = 3.
name: Degree of DE
subject: maths

text: The latus rectum of parabola \( y^2=4ax \) has length:
explanation: Length of latus rectum of \( y^2=4ax \) is \( 4a \) (chord through focus perpendicular to axis).
name: Latus Rectum of Parabola
subject: maths

text: \( \cos\theta + i\sin\theta \) can also be written as:
explanation: By Euler's formula: \( e^{i\theta} = \cos\theta + i\sin\theta \)
name: Euler's Formula
subject: maths

text: The Gattermann reaction is a modification of Sandmeyer reaction using:
options: Cu powder (instead of CuX salt) + HX gas
explanation: Gattermann reaction: ArN₂⁺ + Cu powder + HX → ArX + N₂. Uses Cu metal + HX instead of CuX salt (Sandmeyer).
name: Gattermann Reaction
subject: che

text: Which of the following has the highest bond order?
explanation: Bond order: \(\text{N}_2=3\) (triple), \(\text{O}_2=2\) (double), \(\text{F}_2=1\) (single), \(\text{H}_2=1\). \(\text{N}_2\) has highest.
name: Highest Bond Order
subject: che

text: A galvanometer is converted to a voltmeter by connecting:
explanation: Voltmeter: high resistance \(R\) in series with galvanometer. \( V = I_g(G + R) \Rightarrow R = V/I_g - G \). High resistance so very little current drawn.
name: Galvanometer to Voltmeter
subject: phy

text: Geometric isomerism (cis-trans) is possible in square planar complexes of type:
explanation: cis-trans isomers in square planar: MA₂B₂ type. E.g., [Pt(NH₃)₂Cl₂]: cis (cisplatin, anticancer) and trans isomers.
name: Geometric Isomerism
subject: che

text: In a damped oscillation, the amplitude:
explanation: Damped oscillation: \( A(t) = A_0 e^{-bt/2m} \). Amplitude decreases exponentially due to energy loss (friction/viscosity).
name: Damped Oscillation Amplitude
subject: phy

text: Bond dissociation enthalpy is used to calculate \( \Delta H_{rxn} \) as:
explanation: \( \Delta H = \sum BE_{\text{bonds broken}} - \sum BE_{\text{bonds formed}} \). Breaking bonds absorbs energy; forming bonds releases it.
name: ΔH from Bond Energies
subject: che

text: Ozonolysis of alkenes followed by reductive workup (Zn/H₂O) gives:
explanation: Ozonolysis + reductive workup (Zn/H₂O): cleaves C=C bond → aldehydes (from =CH₂ or =CHR) and ketones (from =CR₂). Oxidative workup (H₂O₂) → carboxylic acids.
name: Ozonolysis Products
subject: che

text: If \( \alpha, \beta \) are roots of \( x^2 - px + q = 0 \), then \( \alpha^2 + \beta^2 = \)
explanation: \( \alpha^2 + \beta^2 = (\alpha+\beta)^2 - 2\alpha\beta = p^2 - 2q \)
name: Sum of Squares of Roots
subject: maths

text: Reduction of a nitrile (R–CN) with LiAlH₄ gives:
explanation: R–CN + LiAlH₄ → R–CH₂–NH₂ (primary amine). The nitrile carbon is reduced to –CH₂–. Useful synthetic route.
name: Reduction of Nitrile
subject: che

text: The value of \( A \) in \( \frac{1}{(x-1)(x+3)} = \frac{A}{x-1} + \frac{B}{x+3} \) is:
explanation: At \(x=1\): \( 1 = A(4) \Rightarrow A = \frac{1}{4} \)
name: Coefficient by Cover-up
subject: maths

text: The second ionisation energy is always greater than the first because:
options: Second electron is in higher shell
Removing electron from positive ion requires more energy
explanation: After removing first electron, the cation has higher effective nuclear charge per electron → second IE > first IE always.
name: Second vs First IE
subject: che

text: By induction, \( n^3 + 2n \) is divisible by:
explanation: \( n^3+2n = n(n^2+2) \). Among 3 consecutive integers, one is divisible by 3; this expression factors to always be divisible by 3.
name: Divisibility of n³+2n
subject: maths

text: The magnetic field at the centre of a circular loop of radius \(R\) carrying current \(I\) is:
explanation: Centre of circular loop: \( B = \frac{\mu_0 I}{2R} \). For N turns: \( B = \frac{\mu_0 NI}{2R} \).
name: Field at Centre of Circular Loop
subject: phy

text: Huygens' principle states that every point on a wavefront acts as:
options: A source of secondary spherical wavelets
explanation: Huygens' principle: each point on a primary wavefront is a source of secondary wavelets. The new wavefront is the envelope of these secondary wavelets.
name: Huygens' Principle
subject: phy

text: Heavy water (D₂O) differs from ordinary water in having:
options: Higher density and higher boiling point
Lower density and lower boiling point
explanation: D₂O: density 1.11 g/mL (vs 1.00 for H₂O), bp 101.4°C (vs 100°C), mp 3.8°C (vs 0°C). Heavier due to deuterium.
name: Heavy Water Properties
subject: che

text: The mobility \(\mu\) of charge carriers is:
options: \( \mu = v_d / E \) (drift velocity per unit electric field)
explanation: Mobility: \( \mu = v_d/E = e\tau/m \). Units: m²/(V·s). Conductivity \( \sigma = ne\mu \).
name: Carrier Mobility
subject: phy

text: The cosine rule gives:
explanation: Cosine Rule: \( a^2 = b^2 + c^2 - 2bc\cos A \)
name: Cosine Rule
subject: maths

text: Optical isomerism (enantiomers) is possible in:
options: Octahedral complexes with three bidentate ligands [M(AA)₃]
explanation: Optical isomers (non-superimposable mirror images) occur in [M(AA)₃] type octahedral complexes (e.g., [Co(en)₃]³⁺). Also [M(AA)₂BC]²⁺.
name: Optical Isomerism in Complexes
subject: che

text: The function \( f(x) = |x| \) is:
explanation: \( f(-x) = |-x| = |x| = f(x) \). So \(f\) is an even function.
name: Even or Odd Function
subject: maths

text: The common chord of two intersecting circles lies on:
explanation: The common chord of two intersecting circles is the radical axis: \( S_1 - S_2 = 0 \).
name: Common Chord
subject: maths

text: The excess pressure inside a soap bubble of radius \(R\) is:
explanation: Soap bubble has 2 surfaces: \( \Delta P = \frac{4T}{R} \). Single liquid drop: \( \Delta P = \frac{2T}{R} \).
name: Excess Pressure in Soap Bubble
subject: phy

text: Which physical quantity has the unit \( \text{kg m}^2\text{s}^{-2} \)?
explanation: Joule (J) = kg m² s⁻². This is the SI unit of energy (and work and torque).
name: Unit of Energy
subject: phy

text: Boron is a metalloid. Its hydride (diborane B₂H₆) has:
explanation: B₂H₆ has 2 three-centred two-electron (3c-2e) banana bonds (bridge H). Electron deficient compound.
name: Diborane Structure
subject: che

text: The radical axis of two circles is the locus of points from which tangents to both circles are:
explanation: The radical axis is the locus of points from which the tangent lengths to both circles are equal.
name: Radical Axis Definition
subject: maths

text: The slope of the \(x\)-axis is:
explanation: The \(x\)-axis is horizontal: slope \( = 0 \). The \(y\)-axis is vertical: slope undefined (\(\infty\)).
name: Slope of Axes
subject: maths

text: The quantity of electricity required to deposit 1 mole of Cu (2+ ion) is:
explanation: \( \text{Cu}^{2+} + 2e^- \to \text{Cu} \). 2 moles of electrons needed = 2F = \( 2 \times 96500 = 193000 \) C.
name: Charge to Deposit Cu
subject: che

text: \( \cos 2\theta = \)
explanation: \( \cos 2\theta = 2\cos^2\theta - 1 = 1 - 2\sin^2\theta = \cos^2\theta - \sin^2\theta \)
name: Double Angle - cos
subject: maths

text: For a second-order reaction \( r = k[A]^2 \), the integrated rate law is:
explanation: Second-order integrated law: \( \frac{1}{[A]} = \frac{1}{[A]_0} + kt \). Plot of \(1/[A]\) vs \(t\) is linear.
name: Second Order Rate Law
subject: che

text: The modulus of \( z = \frac{1+2i}{3-4i} \) is:
name: Modulus of Complex Fraction
subject: maths

text: In an n-type semiconductor, the majority carriers are:
options: Free electrons (from pentavalent donor impurity atoms)
explanation: n-type: doped with Group 15 (pentavalent) elements (P, As, Sb). Extra electron per dopant → electrons are majority carriers. Holes are minority carriers.
name: n-type Majority Carriers
subject: phy

text: The standard equation of a parabola with axis along x-axis is:
explanation: Standard parabola (opens right): \( y^2 = 4ax \), focus at \((a,0)\), directrix \( x = -a \).
name: Parabola Standard Form
subject: maths
